library: HTML/CSS/JavaScript with SVG for visual components
specification:
Type: infographic

Purpose: Illustrate the four key cost components of insurance policies and how they interact

Learning objective: **Understanding** - Explain the relationship between premiums, deductibles, co-payments, and out-of-pocket maximums

Layout: Visual diagram showing a year timeline with cost components stacked

Components:

1. Premium (top layer - continuous)
   - Visual: Horizontal bar spanning full year
   - Color: Blue
   - Label: "Premium: $200/month = $2,400/year"
   - Hover text: "Regular payment you make whether or not you use insurance"

2. Deductible (second layer - early year)
   - Visual: Large rectangle at start of year
   - Color: Orange
   - Label: "Deductible: $1,500"
   - Hover text: "Amount you pay before insurance starts covering costs"

3. Co-payments (third layer - scattered)
   - Visual: Small rectangles scattered across timeline after deductible
   - Color: Yellow
   - Label: "Co-pay: $30 per visit"
   - Hover text: "Fixed amount you pay for each service after meeting deductible"

4. Out-of-Pocket Maximum (boundary line)
   - Visual: Vertical line at a point in timeline
   - Color: Green
   - Label: "Out-of-Pocket Max: $6,000"
   - Hover text: "Maximum you'll pay in a year - insurance covers 100% after this"

Interactive elements:
- Hover over each component to see definition
- Click component to see example scenarios
- Toggle button: "Show Example: $10,000 Surgery"
  - When toggled, show how costs are split between patient and insurance

Example scenario (surgery):
- Premium: Patient always pays ($200/month)
- First $1,500: Patient pays (deductible)
- Next $4,500: Patient pays $900 (20% coinsurance), Insurance pays $3,600
- Out-of-pocket reached: Insurance covers rest 100%
- Patient total: $2,400 (premium) + $1,500 (deductible) + $900 (coinsurance) = $4,800
- Insurance total: $5,200

Implementation: HTML/CSS/JavaScript with SVG for visual components
Canvas size: 800x400px
